posts.title: Show HN: I built an LLM chat app because we shouldn't need 10 AI subscriptions
posts.body:
I'm lost between ChatGPT vs Claude vs Gemini... which subscriptions to take? With Cursor and all these specific AI tools, I just wanted one simple chat app where I can use any model and pay only when I use it.

Couldn't find one, so I built one.

Pay only for what you use. Your prompts and docs, knowledge bases work with every model - no more copy-pasting between apps.

Started as a personal project, but thought someone else might benefit from this too.

https://prismharmony.com/chat

What do you think?

comments.author: Simulacra
comments.body: One improvement that would interesting, if not in there already, is to compare the answers from the different LLMs, and then combine them into the highest probability statement.

comments.author: woodylondon
comments.body:
Good idea, and I also explored this idea and a year ago and also started building one, recognising a gap in the market for a solution that supports multiple LLMs, but also provides small businesses with a centralised managed AI client - billing, monitoring, logging, company prompts, etc.

Ultimately, I discovered https://www.typingmind.com, which offers all of these features. I am sure there are others - I was amazed that not more of these came out. Might be worth to see what they have built. The more of these that come out the better - its a whole new market.

comments.author: iLoveOncall
comments.body: You should integrate with AWS bedrock, you'd be able to offer 60 models instantly without having to spend any time integrating with separate APIs: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/models-...

comments.author: shinycode
comments.body: I think this does the same as well https://mammouth.ai/

comments.author: warthog
comments.body: Just tried it but the messages keep disappearing after submission and streaming.

comments.author: supriyo-biswas
comments.body: I believe there's a couple of similar apps like https://msty.app and https://jan.ai that do the same and allow you to plug in your own API keys.

comments.author: Alifatisk
comments.body: Isn't this what openrouter is for?

comments.author: covercash
comments.body: Tangentially related, but is there any sort of chat bot that will look at the query and suggest which LLM might be the best for that particular task?

triage.classifier_note: Multi-model aggregator; implies fragmentation across Claude and competitors.
